metadata.description: External sources for Mathlib coverage gaps — other Lean libraries, other formal systems, and natural language references

sources.reference: Etingof et al., 'Introduction to Representation Theory' — Definition 2.3.8 and surrounding discussion
sources.relevance: The book itself provides the definition; formalization requires defining indecomposability for modules as non-existence of complementary submodule decomposition

sources.reference: MathComp (Coq) — mathcomp-analysis and mathcomp-ssreflect have module decomposition infrastructure
sources.relevance: Coq's approach to direct sum decomposition may guide the Lean formalization of indecomposable modules

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' (Springer, 2014) — Chapter 2
sources.relevance: Comprehensive treatment of quiver representations as functors from path categories to vector spaces; detailed constructions suitable for formalization

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, 'Elements of the Representation Theory of Associative Algebras, Vol. 1' — Chapter III
sources.relevance: Detailed development of quiver representations with explicit constructions

sources.reference: Coq UniMath — CategoryTheory library has functor categories that model quiver representations
sources.relevance: Demonstrates how to model quiver representations via functors in a proof assistant

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 2.3
sources.relevance: Explicit construction of path algebra as free k-algebra on paths with concatenation; basis theorem

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section III.1
sources.relevance: Path algebra construction with detailed proofs of algebra properties

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 2.4
sources.relevance: Subrepresentations defined as subfunctors; explicit vector subspace conditions at each vertex

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 2.4
sources.relevance: Direct sum constructed vertex-wise and arrow-wise

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 2.5
sources.relevance: Morphisms as families of linear maps commuting with edge maps; kernel/cokernel/image constructions

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.4 (composition series and filtrations)
sources.relevance: Filtrations as ascending chains of submodules; connection to composition series and Jordan-Hölder

sources.reference: Lang, 'Algebra' — Chapter III, Section 4
sources.relevance: Standard treatment of module filtrations in a graduate algebra textbook

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 1.3
sources.relevance: Unitary representations for finite groups; averaging inner product construction; detailed proof of complete reducibility via unitarizability

sources.reference: Fulton & Harris, 'Representation Theory: A First Course' — Section 1.1
sources.relevance: Introductory treatment of unitary representations with explicit constructions

sources.reference: Curtis, 'Pioneers of Representation Theory' — Chapter 1
sources.relevance: Historical development of the group determinant; Frobenius's original factorization theorem

sources.reference: Johnson, 'The Group Determinant' (LMS Lecture Notes) — Chapters 1-3
sources.relevance: Dedicated monograph on group determinants; detailed proofs of factorization over characters

definition_gaps.title: Complex, real, and quaternionic type of irreducible representations

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 13.2
sources.relevance: Classification of irreducible representations into real/complex/quaternionic types via Frobenius-Schur indicator; complete proofs

sources.reference: Fulton & Harris, 'Representation Theory' — Section 3.5
sources.relevance: Real and quaternionic structures on complex representations

sources.reference: MathComp (Coq) — character.v has Frobenius-Schur indicator and real/complex classification
sources.relevance: Complete formalization in Coq; can guide Lean approach to Frobenius-Schur classification

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 13.2
sources.relevance: Definition and computation of ν(χ) = (1/|G|) Σ χ(g²); characterization as 0, 1, or -1

sources.reference: MathComp (Coq) — character.v, cfun_indicator
sources.relevance: Frobenius-Schur indicator formalized in Coq's character theory library

definition_gaps.title: Algebraic number and algebraic integer via eigenvalues

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 6.4
sources.relevance: Characters take values in algebraic integers; eigenvalue characterization

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 9.1
sources.relevance: Representation ring (Grothendieck group of finite-dimensional representations); virtual characters as integer combinations of irreducible characters

sources.reference: MathComp (Coq) — vcharacter.v
sources.relevance: Virtual characters formalized as Z-linear combinations of irreducible characters

sources.reference: Fulton, 'Young Tableaux' (LMS Student Texts) — Chapter 1-2
sources.relevance: Comprehensive development of Young tableaux, semistandard tableaux, and Kostka numbers with combinatorial proofs

sources.reference: Stanley, 'Enumerative Combinatorics Vol. 2' — Section 7.10
sources.relevance: Kostka numbers, Schur polynomials, and their combinatorial properties

sources.reference: Sagan, 'The Symmetric Group' — Chapter 2
sources.relevance: Young tableaux and Kostka numbers in context of symmetric group representations

sources.reference: Green, 'Polynomial Representations of GL_n' (Springer LNM 830) — Chapters 1-3
sources.relevance: Foundational text on polynomial representations; Schur algebras and Schur-Weyl duality

sources.reference: Fulton & Harris, 'Representation Theory' — Part II, Chapters 15 and 23
sources.relevance: Polynomial representations of GL(n) and Schur-Weyl duality with detailed proofs

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Chapter 4
sources.relevance: Dynkin diagrams in context of Gabriel's theorem; classification of positive-definite graphs

sources.reference: Humphreys, 'Introduction to Lie Algebras and Representation Theory' — Section 11
sources.relevance: Classification of Dynkin diagrams via Cartan matrices

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.3
sources.relevance: Roots of quivers defined via the Tits quadratic form; real and imaginary roots

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.3
sources.relevance: Simple roots as standard basis vectors corresponding to quiver vertices

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.3
sources.relevance: Positive roots as dimension vectors of indecomposable representations (Gabriel's theorem)

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 2.2
sources.relevance: Dimension vector as function assigning dim(V_i) to each vertex; straightforward definition

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.5
sources.relevance: Sinks and sources defined; used in BGP reflection functors

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section VII.5
sources.relevance: BGP reflection functors at sinks and sources

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.5
sources.relevance: Local reversal of arrows at a sink/source vertex

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.5
sources.relevance: BGP reflection functors: explicit construction via kernel/cokernel at sink vertices; proof that they preserve indecomposables

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section VII.5
sources.relevance: Detailed construction with full proofs of functoriality and exactness properties

sources.reference: Bernstein, Gelfand, Ponomarev, 'Coxeter functors and Gabriel's theorem' (1973)
sources.relevance: Original paper; freely available; foundational proofs

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.5
sources.relevance: Dual construction at source vertices; same references as F_i^+

sources.reference: Schiffler, 'Quiver Representations' — Section 4.6
sources.relevance: Coxeter element as product of simple reflections; Coxeter functors as composition of reflection functors

sources.reference: Humphreys, 'Reflection Groups and Coxeter Groups' — Chapter 3
sources.relevance: General theory of Coxeter elements in Coxeter groups

sources.reference: Etingof et al., 'Tensor Categories' (AMS Mathematical Surveys, 2015) — Chapter 1
sources.relevance: Semisimple categories defined; relationship to semisimple algebras and module categories

sources.reference: Weibel, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Chapter 3
sources.relevance: Tor as derived functor of tensor product; long exact sequences; explicit computations

sources.reference: Rotman, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Chapter 7
sources.relevance: Accessible treatment with detailed proofs of Tor properties

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.4
sources.relevance: Orthogonal idempotent decompositions; primitive idempotents; connection to indecomposable projectives

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.5
sources.relevance: Projective covers: existence for finitely generated modules over artinian algebras; uniqueness proof

sources.reference: Anderson & Fuller, 'Rings and Categories of Modules' — Chapter 5
sources.relevance: Comprehensive treatment of projective covers and essential epimorphisms

definition_gaps.title: Cartan matrix (of a finite dimensional algebra)

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.4
sources.relevance: Cartan matrix C_A where c_{ij} = multiplicity of S_j in composition series of P_i; properties and examples

sources.reference: Auslander, Reiten, Smalø, 'Representation Theory of Artin Algebras' — Chapter II
sources.relevance: Cartan matrix in context of Artin algebras; relationship to projective-injective duality

sources.reference: Weibel, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Section 4.1
sources.relevance: Projective dimension as length of minimal projective resolution; characterization via Ext vanishing

sources.reference: Rotman, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Chapter 8
sources.relevance: Projective dimension with detailed proofs and examples

sources.reference: Weibel, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Section 4.1
sources.relevance: Global dimension as supremum of projective dimensions; finitistic dimension

sources.reference: Auslander, Reiten, Smalø, 'Representation Theory of Artin Algebras' — Chapter IV
sources.relevance: Homological dimension in context of representation theory

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.6
sources.relevance: Block decomposition of module categories; linkage via Ext; complete proofs

sources.reference: Benson, 'Representations and Cohomology, Vol. 1' — Chapter 1
sources.relevance: Blocks of group algebras with detailed treatment

sources.reference: Etingof et al., 'Tensor Categories' — Section 1.8
sources.relevance: Finite abelian categories defined with all required finiteness conditions; examples

sources.reference: Anderson & Fuller, 'Rings and Categories of Modules' — Chapter 6
sources.relevance: Progenerators and Morita theory; detailed proofs of equivalence criteria

sources.reference: Anderson & Fuller, 'Rings and Categories of Modules' — Chapter 6
sources.relevance: Complete development of Morita theory: equivalences of module categories, progenerators, bimodule characterization

sources.reference: Lam, 'A First Course in Noncommutative Rings' — Chapter 18
sources.relevance: Accessible introduction to Morita equivalence with examples

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.6
sources.relevance: Basic algebras defined; every finite-dimensional algebra is Morita equivalent to a basic algebra; proof via idempotent reduction

sources.reference: Auslander, Reiten, Smalø, 'Representation Theory of Artin Algebras' — Chapter I
sources.relevance: Basic algebras and their quiver descriptions

external_dependency_gaps.description: Finite abelian groups: classification by elementary divisors

sources.reference: Lang, 'Algebra' — Chapter I, Section 10
sources.relevance: Structure theorem for finitely generated abelian groups with full proof

external_dependency_gaps.description: Combinatorics of partitions and Young diagrams

sources.reference: Fulton, 'Young Tableaux' — Chapters 1-4
sources.relevance: Comprehensive development: partitions, Young diagrams, standard/semistandard tableaux, hook lengths, Robinson-Schensted correspondence

sources.reference: Sagan, 'The Symmetric Group' — Chapter 2
sources.relevance: Young tableaux and their combinatorics in context of symmetric group representations

sources.reference: Mathlib Nat.Partition and YoungDiagram — partial coverage
sources.relevance: Partitions and Young diagrams exist; tableaux, hook lengths, content need to be built on top

sources.reference: Bröcker & tom Dieck, 'Representations of Compact Lie Groups' — Chapter I
sources.relevance: Topological groups and compact groups; the book's needs are mostly topological, not smooth manifold structure

sources.reference: Macdonald, 'Symmetric Functions and Hall Polynomials' — Chapter I
sources.relevance: Definitive reference for symmetric polynomials, Schur functions, Newton's identities, Kostka numbers

sources.reference: Stanley, 'Enumerative Combinatorics Vol. 2' — Chapter 7
sources.relevance: Comprehensive treatment of symmetric functions with combinatorial proofs

sources.reference: Lang, 'Algebra' — Chapter III, Section 3
sources.relevance: Standard proof of Jordan-Hölder for modules; the lattice-theoretic approach in Mathlib needs to be connected to modules

sources.reference: Lam, 'A First Course in Noncommutative Rings' — Chapter 1
sources.relevance: Complete proof of Wedderburn-Artin with matrix ring decomposition; very detailed

sources.reference: MathComp (Coq) — mxalgebra.v has some Wedderburn-type decompositions for matrix algebras
sources.relevance: Partial formal coverage in Coq

external_dependency_gaps.description: Classification of irreducible representations of sl(2)

sources.reference: Humphreys, 'Introduction to Lie Algebras and Representation Theory' — Section 7
sources.relevance: Complete classification with highest weight theory; detailed proofs

sources.reference: Fulton & Harris, 'Representation Theory' — Chapter 11
sources.relevance: sl(2) representation theory with explicit constructions

external_dependency_gaps.description: Algebraic conjugates of roots of unity are roots of unity

sources.reference: Washington, 'Introduction to Cyclotomic Fields' — Chapter 1
sources.relevance: Galois theory of cyclotomic fields; conjugates of roots of unity are roots of unity via minimal polynomial

external_dependency_gaps.description: Every module is a quotient of a free module

sources.reference: Rotman, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Section 3.1
sources.relevance: Standard construction; surjection from free module on generators

external_dependency_gaps.description: Characters of representations are class functions

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 2.1
sources.relevance: Character theory foundations; class functions; orthogonality relations; complete proofs

sources.reference: MathComp (Coq) — character.v, classfun.v
sources.relevance: Complete formalization of character theory: class functions, orthogonality, character tables

sources.reference: Lam, 'A First Course in Noncommutative Rings' — Chapter 2
sources.relevance: Nilpotent ideals, Jacobson radical, Nakayama lemma; detailed treatment

sources.reference: Assem, Simson, Skowroński, Vol. 1 — Section I.4
sources.relevance: Orthogonal idempotent decomposition, primitive idempotents, lifting modulo radical

sources.reference: Serre, 'Linear Representations of Finite Groups' — Section 1.3
sources.relevance: Averaging operator and proof of Maschke's theorem via invariant inner product

sources.reference: Humphreys, 'Introduction to Lie Algebras and Representation Theory' — Section 17
sources.relevance: PBW theorem with full proof; ordered monomial basis for U(g)

sources.reference: Dixmier, 'Enveloping Algebras' — Chapter 2
sources.relevance: Multiple proofs of PBW including filtration approach

sources.reference: Weibel, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Chapter 3
sources.relevance: Complete development of Ext: derived functors, long exact sequences, Yoneda's interpretation

sources.reference: Rotman, 'An Introduction to Homological Algebra' — Chapters 6-7
sources.relevance: Accessible treatment with many examples
